subject: [PATCH v1 1/1] mm: start background writeback based on per-wb threshold for strictlimit BDIs

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Andrew Morton noted that the original changelog had useful information on performance impact, and requested that future changelogs be more descriptive of fix importance.

Reviewer Andrew Morton questioned the relevance of a specific comment block in the patch, noting that it is not present in his copy of balance_dirty_pages() and suggesting that the author check current Linus mainline to determine where the information should be added.
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks.

Your v1 patch's changelog had useful information showing the
performance impact on fuse.  I've pasted one sentence from that
changelog into my copy of this changelog, but please don't be sparing
in describing the importance of a fix.

Also, we lost the Fixes tag.  I've added

Fixes: 64dd89ae01f2 ("mm/block/fs: remove laptop_mode") 
Cc: <stable@vger.kernel.org>

to my copy of this v2 patch.  Please confirm that this is appropriate.

---

My copy of balance_dirty_pages() doesn't have this comment block.  I
assume you're patching a different kernel version.  I dropped this
hunk.  Please check current Linus mainline and see if there's somewhere
else where you'd like to add this information.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton noted that a [0/N] message is unnecessary for a single-patch submission, suggesting to include all information in the changelog and placing transient info below the patch's separator.
sentiment_signals: neutral comment about patch submission format
reviews.raw_body:
argh, sorry, I didn't read your [0/N].  Ignore.

btw, a [0/N] isn't really needed for a singleton patch - best to just
put everything into the patch's changelog.

Transient info such as "this depends on my other patch" can be placed
below the singleton patch's "---" separator.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in their previous patch and agreed to make changes in future revisions.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah I see, I will do that instead next time, sorry for the confusion.

Thanks,
Joanne

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch improves the proactive start of background writeback for strictlimit BDIs, but suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Thanks for improving these! Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-03-26.patch_summary: This patch modifies the balance_dirty_pages function to start background writeback based on a per-writer (per-wb) threshold for strictlimit block devices, such as fuse. This ensures that background writeback is started when the writer's dirty count exceeds its background threshold, even if the global dirty threshold has not been reached yet. The patch addresses a performance issue where background writeback was not being proactively started for strictlimit BDIs, leading to stalls in the throttle loop waiting for dirty pages to be written back. The patch includes a test case that shows a 3-4% improvement in performance for buffered writes using fio on fuse.
